mnemonics.title: Design Guidelines
mnemonics.content: SRD: Semantic integrity, Reduce Redundancy, Decrease Deletion anomalies (alliteration helps remember the 3 main guidelines)

mnemonics.title: Normal Forms Progression
mnemonics.content: 1NF: No Repeating Groups → 2NF: No Partial Dependencies → 3NF: No Transitive Dependencies → BCNF: Superkey for every FD (mnemonic: No Partial, No Transitive, Superkey is KEY)

mnemonics.title: Anomalies Types
mnemonics.content: I-D-U: Insert, Delete, Update - remember these three problems caused by poor design

mnemonics.title: Lossless Join Check
mnemonics.content: Common attributes form key - think 'Intersection must identify' for lossless join

acronyms.usage: A stricter form of 3NF where every determinant must be a candidate key
A constraint between two sets of attributes in a relation
An attribute that references the primary key of another relation
The unique identifier for each tuple in a relation
Represents absence of a value in an attribute
A normal form that eliminates transitive dependencies
A normal form that eliminates partial dependencies

visualTips.visualization: Think of FD as a one-way arrow: X → Y means 'knowing X tells you Y' - like a function f(x)=y in mathematics
Picture two circles (R1 and R2) overlapping - the intersection area (common attributes) must be the 'glue' (key) that perfectly reconstructs the original
Redundancy as multiple copies of same paper - update one but forget others = inconsistency (Update anomaly)
Concentric circles - outermost is superkey, inner minimal circle is candidate key, innermost is primary key

analogies.analogy: Like a fingerprint - knowing the person (X) uniquely identifies their fingerprint (Y)
Organizing a library - books (data) organized by genre (normal forms) to find them easily and avoid duplicate copies
Like two puzzle pieces that perfectly fit together - when combined, they recreate the exact original picture with no missing or extra pieces
False positive in a medical test - appears to show a condition that doesn't actually exist
Traffic rules being enforced locally - each locality enforces its own rules and collectively all original rules are covered
